item.title: India AI Startup Sarvam Raises Funds at $1.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: India’s homegrown artificial intelligence startup, Sarvam AI, is close to raising $300 million to $350 million as it seeks to build a domestic player that can compete with leaders from the US and China.

item.title: Amazon Imposes 3.5% Fuel Surcharge for Many Online Merchants
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. will start charging sellers who use its shipping services a 3.5% “fuel and logistics” surcharge later this month, joining the ranks of shipping companies raising prices as the war in Iran pushes oil prices higher.

item.title: Aegea Bonds Hit as Traders Sell First, Ask Later in Brazil Rout
item.description: In a matter of weeks, Aegea Saneamento e Participacoes SA went from eyeing a multi-billion-dollar valuation in a Brazilian IPO to watching its bond investors rush for the exits.

item.title: NASA Astronauts Gearing Up for Final Engine Push in Path to Moon
item.description: A NASA crew of four astronauts are preparing for a critical stage of their journey that will set them on course for the moon, a step that will ultimately bring them closer to the lunar surface than anyone has been in more than 50 years.

item.title: US Lawmakers Propose Crackdown on Sales of Chip Tools to China
item.description: US lawmakers unveiled legislation that seeks to crack down on exports of chipmaking tools to China, especially from allies including the Netherlands and Japan, in a bipartisan effort to help Washington further constrain Beijing’s technology ambitions.

item.title: Nordstrom’s Sales Return to 2019 Levels After Deal to Go Private
item.description: Nordstrom’s revenue has returned to its pre-pandemic peak less than a year after the department store went private in a $6.25 billion deal, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fighting US Tariffs, Canada Mulls Its Own as Cheap Imports Surge
item.description: Canadian officials are considering unusual measures to protect domestic producers of vegetables and wood products from low-priced imports.

item.title: Egypt Holds Rates After Iran War Hits Pound, Drives Fuel Surge
item.description: Egypt kept interest rates unchanged, seeking to tame the impact of the US-Israel war on Iran that’s sent energy prices soaring and the pound to a record low.

item.title: Carson Block Is Hedging AI Meltdown By Betting Against Credit ETFs
item.description: Block bets against HYG, LQD on AI job loss fears

item.title: German Investor Pain Spreads to London With Losses From Olympia
item.description: Versicherungskammer Bayern flagged millions of euros in losses linked to one of London’s biggest property developments, adding to already painful hits for the German insurer from a foray into US real estate.

item.title: Heathrow Airport Unit Is Eying Its Latest Bond Sale in Canada
item.description: A unit of Heathrow Airport is looking to raise around C$500 million ($359 million) through a bond sale in Canada, according to people familiar with the matter, amid improved market conditions there for overseas borrowers.

item.title: Russia Testing US Energy Blockade of Cuba With Second Oil Tanker
item.description: Russia plans to send a second oil tanker to crisis-ridden Cuba, further testing an effective US energy blockade of the communist-run island.

item.title: Blackstone Squeezes Thoma Bravo and Its Ailing Software Company Medallia
item.description: A group of private credit firms led by Blackstone Inc. has refused to extend another lifeline to software company Medallia, amping up pressure on owner Thoma Bravo to inject more equity into the troubled business or hand over the keys via a debt restructuring.

item.title: T. Rowe Price, Loomis Are Buying MBS That Have Grown Cheap
item.description: Money managers including T. Rowe Price Group Inc. are looking for bargains among mortgage bonds after the war in Iran and gyrating bond yields clobbered the securities over the last month.

item.title: The Impressively Resilient US Markets
item.description: Nothing’s really down very much.

item.title: First Prediction Market Note Offers Nvidia Bets With Less Risk
item.description: Investors who like the binary bets on Kalshi and Polymarket but without the all-or-nothing risk are being offered an unlikely new asset: a bond-like note tied to prediction market outcomes.

item.title: Iranian Oil Prices Fetch a Premium for First Time Since 2022
item.description: Iranian oil is trading at a premium to the global Brent crude benchmark for the first time since May 2022, a sharp reversal from the steep discounts that have typically defined its sanctions-constrained trade.

item.title: Barrick Taps Goldman Sachs to Lead IPO for North American Mines
item.description: Barrick Mining Corp. has tapped Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to lead an initial public offering of its North American assets, according to people familiar with the matter, as the company moves forward with plans to spin out its premier gold mines.

item.title: A Bottle of Wine Shows the Slow-Motion Impact of Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: Prices are rising despite efforts by vintners, importers and distributors to keep them in check.

item.title: Private Credit’s CLO Machine Ramps Up in Push to Raise More Cash
item.description: Faced with a wave of redemptions and market turmoil, the private credit industry is leaning into a classic Wall Street maneuver: securitization.

item.title: Trump Threats on Iran Civilian Sites Raise Escalation Fears
item.description: President Donald Trump’s repeated threat to destroy Iranian civilian infrastructure is the latest sign of the US leader’s increasing detachment from international norms and the bounds of modern warfare, a trend that’s alienating traditional allies.

item.title: Brazil Rig Supplier Expects Iran War to Drive Latam Oil Drilling
item.description: A leading supplier of deep-water drilling rigs to Brazil’s national oil company expects the industry to favor oil and gas projects in Latin America, where geopolitical risk is lower than the Middle East.

item.title: War Helps Boost an African Nation’s Gas Price Over $14 a Gallon
item.description: Malawi raised its petrol price to 6,672 kwacha ($3.86) per liter, more than three times what US drivers are fretting over having to pay at the pump as oil surges due to the Iran war.

item.title: Russia Baltic Crude Terminal Looks Undamaged in Satellite Images
item.description: The crude terminal at Russia’s Baltic port of Ust-Luga appears undamaged in satellite images after multiple Ukrainian drone attacks in the region.

item.title: Argentina to Maintain Gas Prices in Shift for Free-Market Milei
item.description: Argentina’s state-run energy firm YPF SA will keep gas prices at the pump steady for 45 days in a break from President Javier Milei’s free-market approach as the conflict in Iran adds to inflationary pressures.

item.title: Logan Says Fed Can Shrink Balance Sheet by Changing Regulations
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan said the US central bank could shrink its balance sheet through regulatory changes that reduce banks’ demand for reserves.

item.title: Startup Doctolib’s Valuation Slides 38% in Secondary Share Sale
item.description: Employees and early investors in French health-care startup Doctolib have sold shares in the company worth about €300 million ($345 million), the company said.

item.title: Fewer Americans Plan to Travel After Surging Fuel Costs Hit Budgets
item.description: Travelers say they’re cutting back on international trips, shortening stays and “buckling down.”

item.title: Microsoft Aims to Create Large Cutting-Edge AI Models By 2027
item.description: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year, part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most powerful AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: DOJ to Appeal Court Order Halting Trump’s Ban on Anthropic AI
item.description: The Trump administration will appeal a federal judge’s order blocking its ban on government use of Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence technology.
